summary: Listener is a first-time Rockville Democratic primary voter for June 23, 2026. As of today (2026-05-16) the SBE candidate list is finalized — 40+ Democratic primary decisions on the ballot. The Friedson deep dive shipped today, directly addressing the listener's three highest-priority recurring asks (sanctuary policy, fiscal record, Wootton). The new highest-value work is per-candidate deep dives on the remaining four Democratic candidates for County Executive (Banerjee, Glass, James, Jawando), the seventeen-name At-Large field (especially Elrich and Silvestre), and Kramer's career-long Senate record. Side-by-side policy comparison across the listener's ballot is now possible and is the deliverable to build toward.
themes: Per-candidate, bills-and-votes treatment for every Democrat on the listener's ballot — gating constraint is 38 days to primary, 22 unique races, 60+ candidates total once tier-3/tier-4 fields are included.
Side-by-side policy comparison across MoCo Executive primary candidates on listener's three flagged topics (sanctuary, fiscal/property tax, Wootton).

Treatment of Council At-Large field as the highest-leverage race on the ballot (17 candidates for 4 slots; most-open race in a decade) — needs structural overview plus per-candidate work prioritized by Elrich (former county exec), Silvestre (sitting BoE president), Sayles (incumbent), Fani-González (incumbent).

Kramer career-long Senate voting record as a multi-episode deep dive (D19 Senate is uncontested — the listener's yes-or-skip vote is informed by the historical record, not the absence of a primary opponent).
MoCo capital-budget mechanism as the executive's only direct lever on Wootton — needs revisiting whenever a county-exec candidate's school position is discussed.
open_questions: Glass and Jawando deep dives (each with explicit Wootton, sanctuary, fiscal-record positions documented).
Mithun Banerjee and Peter James biographies and policy positions — currently no public record we have surfaced.
Kramer's career-long voting record beyond recent sessions (multi-episode treatment).
Where the five Circuit Court judicial candidates differ in judicial philosophy or sitting-vs-challenger status.
Per-candidate research on the lower-profile At-Large names (Barrie, Caballero, Chowdhury, Gassaway, Goldberg, Khan, Losak, McNulty, Pope, Selvam, Solomon, True, Vergagni, Wali).
underserved_topics: FY27 county budget gap and how each MoCo Executive candidate proposes to close it in named dollar terms and named program cuts.
Sheriff race between Will Milam and Maxwell Cornelius Uy — neither has been covered.
Register of Wills race (Bowser/Dollahite/Ebel) — function of the office plus candidate backgrounds.
Down-ballot judicial race (Sixth Judicial Circuit, 5 candidates for 4 slots) — judicial-selection process explained for first-time voter.
Wootton High School position from Glass, Jawando, Banerjee, and James — only Friedson is on the record.
avoid_list: The 'four for four against Elrich property-tax increases (2020, 2023, 2025, 2026)' Friedson framing — used as the central fiscal-discipline summary in the 2026-05-16 deepdive_friedson and ep1; do not repeat verbatim in subsequent Friedson references or in other county-executive deep dives.

The 'October 28 2025 Wootton testimony / Crown relocation / Rockville Mayor Monique Ashton and Adam Van Grack credited' Friedson-on-Wootton framing — used in the 2026-05-16 deepdive_friedson. For Glass/Jawando/James/Banerjee Wootton coverage, lead from their own records, not by contrasting against this Friedson framing.

The April 28 2026 'permanent police officers in every high school' Friedson letter as the lead public-safety hook — used in the 2026-05-16 deepdive_friedson and ep1. Re-using as a primary frame in upcoming Glass or Jawando episodes would feel like Friedson-centric coverage. Use it as comparison context only.

The 'ballot is officially final / 40-plus candidate decisions / top-to-bottom ballot read' framing — used in 2026-05-16 ep1 (filing_monitor finalized the list this morning). Do not lead future episodes with a 'the ballot just got final' reveal — it has now been disclosed.

The 'seventeen At-Large candidates competing for four seats — most open At-Large race in a decade — Jawando and Glass moved out, Elrich moved in' At-Large structural framing — used in 2026-05-16 ep1 and ep2. Do not re-narrate the seat structure; assume the listener heard it. Move directly to per-candidate substance in subsequent At-Large coverage.

The CASA-endorses-Ferguson hook used to connect state Senate leadership to county-level Trust Act / County Values Act — used in 2026-05-16 ep2. Find a different connective tissue when the next state-senate leadership story comes through.

Kanaiyah's Law foster-care framing introduced in 2026-05-16 ep3 in the Moore bill-signing roundup — do not lead another episode with Kanaiyah's Law specifically; deeper child-welfare coverage should come from a different angle (e.g., MoCo Health and Human Services implementation).
The framing 'a listener asked us last week/earlier this week which Democratic primary candidates actually oppose sanctuary policy on illegal immigration' as a setup line — overused
The Evan Glass DHS-screening bill as the concrete sanctuary-policy example — already used twice
The ICE mask-ban ordinance as the sanctuary-policy benchmark — already used
'Two stories, properly worked through' / 'two stories, not spreading thin across six' framing — already used
Governor Moore's 'Leave No One Behind' slogan vs disability service cuts — already used twice
The ACA marketplace quiet-downgrade story for Marylanders — already used twice
Maryland's 90-day part-time legislature explanation — already used twice
The wave of 25 Maryland lawmakers retiring or running for other offices framing — already used three times
Beltsville Agricultural Research Center closure as a District 19 candidate-record hook — already used twice
Narrating the listener's own frustration about promised deep-dives back to them — already used in 5+ episodes
